Leading Concord

Aeysha's
Story

Aeysha Corio knows strong communities don't happen by accident. They are built through hard work, thoughtful leadership, and neighbors who care deeply about where they live.

Aeysha chose to move her family to Concord because she wanted a safe community for her family to put down roots. She wanted her adult children to have the option to live close by, and enough economic opportunity to make this a sustainable place to live for everyone in her family.

With Councilmember Pablo Benavente
$25M
Raised for Cancer Support Community

Elected Official Endorsers
East Bay Leadership Council
Leadership & Service

From Curiosity
to Commissioner

As a new resident and local small business owner, Aeysha enrolled in the Concord Community Institute, a program that introduces residents to how Concord works. What began as curiosity quickly turned into a passion and commitment to community service. She joined the Concord Pavilion Community Outreach Committee and was later asked to serve as a Planning Commissioner, where she uses her deep knowledge of land use and planning policy to help guide responsible growth, while protecting the quality of life that make Concord such a special place.

Aeysha is known for her strong work ethic and practical yet creative problem-solving. She believes leadership means listening carefully, bringing people together, and finding solutions that get results. She is deeply committed to public safety and ensuring that families feel secure in their neighborhoods, while keeping Concord a vibrant place to live, work, and raise a family.

Passion for Service

Making Concord
Healthier

Her passion for service also extends as an active leader in several organizations. For example, Aeysha has helped raise $25 million for Cancer Support Community San Francisco Bay Area to fund an innovative community center, the first of its kind in California. She also has served in several leadership positions with John Muir Community Health Fund, where she works to distribute grants and partner with community based non profit organizations to create sustainable health initiatives that address the root cause of poor health in Central and East Contra Costa. Aeysha knows that while some problems are unavoidable, what matters is how you fix the problem, both in a realistic and timely way.

Cancer Support Community SF Bay Area
With Contra Costa County firefighters
Public Safety

Safe Families,
Safe Neighborhoods

Aeysha Corio is a small business owner, a wife to Steve, a mother of four, and a grandmother of two.

"While some problems are unavoidable, what matters is how you fix the problem — both in a realistic and timely way."
Experience & Leadership

What Aeysha Brings to City Council

01
Local Small Business Owner
Aeysha understands firsthand what it takes to build and sustain a business in Concord, committed to economic policies that support local entrepreneurs and families.
02
Planning Commissioner
Uses deep knowledge of land use and planning policy to guide responsible growth while protecting the quality of life that makes Concord a special place to call home.
03
Community Organization Leader
Active leader in Cancer Support Community Bay Area, John Muir Community Health Fund, Concord Pavilion Outreach Committee, and the Concord Community Institute.
